summary: Drew DeVault's June 2021 status update covers progress on SourceHut, his secret programming language (Hare), and IRC tooling. SourceHut gained NetBSD build support, a new GraphQL API for builds.sr.ht, and improved onboarding flows. The secret language hit a point requiring deep compiler refactoring, expected to take months. He also contributed to gamja and soju, IRC client and bouncer projects being adopted by Libera.chat.
key_points: SourceHut added NetBSD build images and a writable GraphQL API for builds.sr.ht, with lists.sr.ht API design underway
The secret programming language (later revealed as Hare) needs deep compiler refactoring before new features can land
Libera.chat adopted gamja as an experimental webchat client, with DeVault helping polish it
Plans to generalize soju IRC bouncer for eventual hosted offering on sr.ht
money_quotes: In the course of implementing it, it became clear to me that we need to take a step back and do some deep refactoring in the compiler.
I've also redesigned the registration & onboarding flow based on a maintainer/contributor distinction, which should help people understand how sourcehut works a bit better.
I'm also working on generalizing soju a bit so that we can eventually utilize it to offer a hosted IRC bouncer for sr.ht users.
Libera.chat is running an experimental instance of gamja for their webchat.
My foci have been on sourcehut and the secret language, and will continue to be those.
key_insight: Steady incremental progress on SourceHut's GraphQL APIs and the secret programming language, with a pragmatic willingness to pause feature work for necessary compiler refactoring.
